other_observers: Sol Miller, on the 19th. None on the 20th;
description: A very large, dark-mantled gull.   Bird clearly as large or slightly larger than any other Herring Gull in the vicinity, and much larger than every Ring-billed Gull. Mantle color dark slaty gray, in sharp contrast with mantle color of every other gull in the area. Very clean white underparts and head, and only minimal smudginess on the nape. Bill yellow, with black 'ring' near tip, and small red gonys spot inward of it. Eye color dim yellow. Small white scapular crescent, actually more like a patch, obvious against the mantle plumage. Small white tips on otherwise black primaries when wings folded. Legs light pink when seen standing on ice edge.Behaviors: Bird mostly swam and foraged in the open water alongside and near other gulls (Ring-billed and Herring) and waterfowl (mostly Common Goldeneye). These 'swim-bys' allowed numerous occasions for size comparisons between birds. Was not particularly aggressive toward other birds around it, even when it appeared they had found edible material.   For short intermittent periods (3-5 minutes) it left the water and stood on the ice, grooming and 'resting'. However, it never stayed out long before resuming its foraging. Mostly stayed in the same open water patch, that is, not exploring a couple sizable neighboring patches.Call: none heardPlumage: adult winter
photographer: Photographer: Eric DeFonso, Eric DeFonso, Eric DeFonso, Eric DeFonso
